compiled_state.author: Iso Tester
compiled_state.description:
Own the Pass-3 **deterministic novelty score + rising-floor drop rule** (task 3). Consume the
task-150b novelty score (`decide.novelty` / `verify.score_novelty`, both landed by 150b) and the
task-ec89 remediation-mode eligibility decision, then apply the deterministic floor. This task
adds the drop math, the narrowed coverage, the sidecar write of dropped findings, the runtime
defaults, and the activation guard. (Note: `NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS`, `decide.novelty`, and
`verify.score_novelty` are 150b deliverables already merged on this branch — a re-review that
grounds against `origin/main` will not see them; they exist in the work branch.)

## Where the floor is applied (Pass-3 stays pure)

The floor is applied in the `_run_plan_review` WRAPPER (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py`),
AFTER the deterministic verdict is produced and the ec89 eligibility decision is computed — NOT
inside `orchestrator.pass3_over_findings` / the `plan_review_decide` workflow step (those stay
pure arithmetic with no LLM). So **no existing caller of `pass3_over_findings` changes**. The
wrapper, when (and only when) eligible AND active, scores novelty over the surfaced advisory
findings (the droppable surface — blocking findings are high-priority and never dropped) via the
150b sub-call, then moves the dropped findings out of `advisory` into the verdict's `dropped`
bucket before the sidecar is emitted.

## Drop rule (deterministic — no LLM holistic severity)

A finding is dropped **iff** `novelty ≥ T_novel` AND `priority < floor`, where
`priority = validity × impact` (the existing Pass-3 score). Carryover findings (novelty < T_novel)
use the normal threshold and must still be resolved. A novel high-priority finding is preserved
(and may block). Implemented as a pure predicate `decide.rising_floor_drop(priority, novelty, *,
t_novel, floor) -> bool` so it is unit-testable in isolation.

## Defaults this task owns (derived, not guessed)

- `verify.novelty_drop_threshold` (`T_novel`) = **0.7** — the runtime drop threshold,
  config-overridable. House precision-first default; DISTINCT from the `discriminates_novelty`
  eval bar (0.5 midpoint, task 150b). It is the documented default; per-corpus calibration is
  explicitly deferred (epic "Defaults & calibration").
- `verify.novelty_priority_floor` = a **scalar** constant (config-overridable), set to the **p40
  impact percentile of the 245-event corpus** (≈ the "below major" band). It is a single number,
  NOT a per-finding adaptive value: the drop test is `priority < floor`, and since
  `priority = validity × impact`, the validity factor is already in `priority` — the floor itself
  is a fixed impact-percentile scalar. `scripts/plan_review_impact_distribution.py` is extended to
  print the impact PERCENTILES (incl. p40) so the chosen number is reproducible, not asserted.

## Activation guard (the evidence gate — explicit mechanism)

Activation is a **config key** `verify.novelty_drop_active` (default **False**). Mechanism (a):
the operator flips it to true MANUALLY after validating that 150b's `discriminates_novelty` eval
has cleared its bar (`rebar prompt eval plan-review-novelty` → the labeled pairs discriminate at
`gate: at_least(2)`/`epochs:3` + `coverage_threshold:0.8`). Until then the gate runs full/
un-floored. The drop path in `_run_plan_review` is reached only when ALL of: config
`remediation_mode` on (ec89), the ec89 eligibility decision `eligible`, AND
`novelty_drop_active` true — so the floor is triple-gated and inert by default. A test asserts the
drop rule is inert while `novelty_drop_active` is False even when eligibility holds.

## Narrowed coverage + sidecar (observability)

On a narrowed verdict the wrapper sets, on `verdict["coverage"]` (an open dict — no schema file
change), `narrowed: true`, `floored_criteria` (the criteria carried by the dropped findings), and
`floored_finding_ids` (the dropped findings' ids). These keys are ABSENT on a normal/un-floored
review (byte-identical to today). Every dropped novel finding is moved into the verdict's
`dropped` bucket, which `sidecar.build_payload` already persists (slimmed, WITH `norm_id`) — so
suppression is always observable and joinable by `norm_id`, and the dropped findings are NOT in
the surfaced verdict's `advisory`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Deterministic `decide.rising_floor_drop(priority, novelty, *, t_novel, floor)`: drop iff `novelty ≥ T_novel` AND `priority < floor`; carryover (novelty < T_novel) → normal threshold; novel high-priority → preserved (`src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py`)
- [ ] `verify.novelty_drop_threshold` (default 0.7) and `verify.novelty_priority_floor` (a scalar = the stated corpus p40 impact percentile) are committed config-overridable defaults in `src/rebar/config.py`; `scripts/plan_review_impact_distribution.py` prints the impact percentiles the floor is derived from
- [ ] Activation guard `verify.novelty_drop_active` (default False, operator-flipped after the 150b eval clears its bar); the floor is inert while False; a test asserts un-floored behavior when the flag is False even though eligibility holds
- [ ] The floor is applied in `_run_plan_review` (post-verdict), gated on ec89 eligibility AND `novelty_drop_active` AND `remediation_mode`; `pass3_over_findings` and the `plan_review_decide` step are unchanged (Pass-3 stays pure)
- [ ] Coverage on a narrowed verdict sets `narrowed: true` + `floored_criteria` + `floored_finding_ids` on `verdict["coverage"]` (absent on a normal review)
- [ ] Every dropped novel finding is moved to the verdict's `dropped` bucket (persisted to the `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar with `norm_id`), and removed from the surfaced `advisory`
- [ ] Consumes task ec89's remediation-mode eligibility decision (`coverage["remediation"]`); does NOT re-implement the freshness-window/precondition check
- [ ] Offline tests (no live LLM): the pure drop predicate (all four quadrants), the inert-when-flag-False guard, the narrowed-coverage assembly + sidecar dropped bucket with an INJECTED novelty map (so no LLM call is needed to prove the wrapper)

file_impact.reason: apply floor post-verdict when eligible+active; narrowed coverage; dropped to sidecar
print impact percentiles so the floor is reproducible
drop predicate, inert-when-flag-false, narrowed coverage + sidecar tests

compiled_state.title: Pass-3: deterministic novelty score + rising-floor drop rule
